title: Gratiola viscidula
taxonomy: Gratiola viscidula is classified within the family Plantaginaceae. This plant is further classified within the genus Gratiola. The species viscidula is a part of the Gratiola genus. Gratiola viscidula is a type of plant native to the Americas. It is known to be a species of flowering plant that belongs to the order Lamiales.
uses: Gratiola viscidula, also known as sticky hedge hyssop, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems have been employed to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Additionally, Gratiola viscidula is believed to have a potential role in reducing fever and alleviating coughs. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.
